reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–H (tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to nitrile oxide), loss of H2O

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are miscible in the DES and stable at room temperature; premixing is acceptable since the reaction only proceeds at elevated temperature. A single feed simplifies pumping of the viscous DES medium.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: DES hydrogen bond acceptor / solvent component
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with ethylene glycol (1 mL total DES)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: DES hydrogen bond donor / solvent component

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) with loss of water, promoted by the DES medium at 120°C
Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate

stream_logic.reasoning: All components are premixed in a single stream. The reaction does not proceed at room temperature, so premixing is safe. The DES is viscous (~50–100 cP), so a single syringe/HPLC pump with appropriate back-pressure is preferred. Two-stream mixing of the viscous DES would be unnecessarily complex with no chemical benefit.

mixing_order_reasoning: Since the cycloaddition only initiates at elevated temperature (120°C), all reagents can be safely premixed at room temperature in the DES. The nitrile oxide intermediate forms only upon heating, so no premature reaction occurs. A single premixed feed stream is optimal given the high viscosity of the DES.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; the batch protocol uses open air conditions.
quench_reasoning: The batch protocol quenches with water for workup/extraction purposes only. In flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No inline quench is chemically necessary.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photoinitiation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES viscosity (~50–100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) is the main flow engineering concern — may require heated feed lines or dilution to ensure pumpability. Back-pressure regulator needed to keep DES/solvent below boiling at 120°C. The 15 min residence time is very flow-compatible. Uncertainty: exact viscosity of TBAB/EG at operating temperature; whether DES can be reliably pumped through narrow-bore tubing without clogging. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests some nitrile oxide is lost to dimerization — flow's superior mixing may allow reduction toward 1.5 equiv.
